disclaimer: Weight-based guide doses for common paediatric medicines, compiled from standard references (IAP/WHO ranges). ALWAYS confirm with your paediatrician — this tool cannot account for your child's full medical picture.

rules.label: Paracetamol (fever / pain)
rules.notes: Do not exceed 4 doses in 24h. Never combine with other paracetamol-containing medicines (many cold syrups contain it).

rules.label: Ibuprofen (fever / pain / inflammation)
rules.notes: Give with food/milk. Avoid if the child is dehydrated (vomiting/loose motions) — kidney risk. Avoid in suspected dengue.

rules.label: Mefenamic acid (fever / pain)
rules.notes: Give with food. Not a first choice for routine fever — paracetamol is safer.

rules.label: Amoxicillin (antibiotic)
rules.frequency: twice or thrice daily as prescribed, for the full course
rules.notes: Antibiotics need a doctor's prescription and the FULL course — never stop when the child feels better.

rules.label: Amoxicillin + Clavulanate (antibiotic)
rules.frequency: twice daily, for the full course (dose counts the amoxicillin part)
rules.notes: Give at the start of a meal to reduce stomach upset. Prescription antibiotic — full course.

rules.label: Azithromycin (antibiotic)
rules.notes: Prescription antibiotic. Give 1h before or 2h after food for best absorption.

rules.label: Cefixime (antibiotic)
rules.frequency: twice daily (8 mg/kg/day total), for the full course

rules.label: Ondansetron (vomiting)
rules.frequency: every 8 hours, only if vomiting continues
rules.notes: For gastro, ORS (rehydration) matters more than stopping vomiting. See a doctor if vomiting persists or the child is drowsy/dehydrated.

rules.label: Domperidone (vomiting / bloating)
rules.frequency: up to three times daily, 15–30 min before food
rules.notes: Use the lowest dose for the shortest time.

rules.label: Metronidazole (amoebiasis / giardia)
rules.frequency: three times daily, for the prescribed course

rules.label: Albendazole (deworming)

fixed_doses.dose_text: 200 mg (½ tablet or 5 ml of 400mg/10ml suspension) as a single dose
400 mg (1 tablet or 10 ml suspension) as a single dose

rules.frequency: single dose (often repeated after 2 weeks — ask your doctor)
rules.notes: Routine deworming is typically every 6 months for children. Whole family is often treated together.
